name: Tianshou (PyTorch-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Tsinghua Machine Learning Group

performance.notes: PyTorch backend; vectorized environments; async data collection

agent_readiness.notes: Elegant PyTorch RL library from Tsinghua. from tianshou.policy import PPOPolicy, SACPolicy, TD3Policy. from tianshou.trainer import OnpolicyTrainer. from tianshou.data import Collector, VectorEnv. envs = DummyVectorEnv([lambda: gym.make('CartPole-v1')]*4). net = Net(state_shape, action_shape). policy = PPOPolicy(actor, critic, optim). collector = Collector(policy, envs). trainer = OnpolicyTrainer(policy, train_collector, test_collector). Algorithms: DQN, DDPG, TD3, SAC, PPO, A2C, IQN, FQF, BCQ. Async data collection. Gymnasium compatible.

gotchas: Less popular than Stable Baselines 3 — smaller community
Documentation examples can lag behind API changes
More complex setup than SB3 for simple use cases
best_when: Research requiring custom RL algorithms in PyTorch
When more flexibility than Stable Baselines 3 is needed
Clean modular RL architecture for academic publication
avoid_when: Production RL deployments (use SB3 for reliability)
Quick benchmarking of standard algorithms (SB3 is simpler)
summary: Tianshou is a PyTorch RL library from Tsinghua with clean modular architecture. PPOPolicy, SACPolicy, TD3Policy. Collector for data collection. OnpolicyTrainer / OffpolicyTrainer. VectorEnv for parallel envs. Gymnasium compatible. Algorithms: DQN, SAC, TD3, PPO, A2C, IQN, BCQ. MIT. More flexible than SB3 but smaller community. Best for RL research requiring custom algorithm modifications.
